note: Cold spec-judge round 1 returned ALIGNED false — builder lens FAIL. Every drafted scenario asserted the guard fails closed; none asserted it raises no violation on input that parses. An implementation hardcoded to always fail closed would satisfy the whole suite, so the corpus sweep's new contract could not distinguish measuring from refusing. This is the safety dual of the fail-open defect the CR exists to close. Closed additively with two discriminating scenarios (clean corpus raises no parse violation and does not fail closed; a parsing touched file raises none).

Round 1 architect observation prompted a re-read that refuted the observation and found a prose defect instead. The README claimed the parse guard fails the tree-wide sweep closed unlike the touched-scope checks around it. There is no such asymmetry: the form check already fails the tree-wide sweep closed today, which is why the corpus check gates CI. Prose corrected to state the guard takes the same path as every other form violation, distinguished only by which parser renders the verdict.

Cold impl-judge round 1 returned CHANGE. Three of fourteen frozen scenarios rested on no automated verification, proven by ablation rather than by reading: reverting the tree-wide sweep to its pre-fix code left the whole suite green, and rewriting the differ's failure branch to return a reassuring class left it green too. Independently reproduced both ablations before accepting the finding. Closed by driving the sweep at the composition level (discovery feeding the validated path, which is where the defect actually lives) and by injecting the differ so its failure branch is reachable from a test.

The first tree-wide sweep test was itself unloseable. Its fixture was both unparseable and hedged, so the sweep failed closed on the hedge even with the parse guard reverted — the test passed without ever measuring the parse verdict. Caught by re-running the ablation against the new test rather than trusting that adding a test closed the gap. Replaced with a fixture the pinned parser rejects while the permissive scan reads it as flawless, so the parse verdict is the only thing that can fail it. The ablation now takes two tests red.

Impl-judge probed the real differ and refuted a comment claiming it pairs a parse error with a per-file addOnly true. The per-file value is false; it is the top-level summary that reads addOnly true. Corrected the comments to name the actual reassuring signal: an unparseable file yields an EMPTY scenarios array, which the per-scenario classifier reads as no-content-change. That emptiness, not addOnly, is what the guard refuses to trust.
